title: Permutation-Invariant Field-Context Expert
summary: Current field context is mostly hand-engineered means/ranks plus a small adjacent-driver proxy, but pit timing is inherently multi-agent.
rationale: Current field context is mostly hand-engineered means/ranks plus a small adjacent-driver proxy, but pit timing is inherently multi-agent. Set-based models are built for permutation-invariant group inputs and recent F1 strategy work explicitly models competitor interaction modules.
implementation_hint: Build a small Deep Sets style expert per `Year-Race-LapNumber`: encode every other car's current state with a shared MLP, sum/mean-pool to a field context vector, optionally add a separate teammate token, then concatenate with the focal driver's features and blend its OOF predictions with the tree model.
expected_effect: Captures pit-wave, undercut, and teammate/opponent interaction patterns that simple race-level aggregates miss, giving a differentiated ensemble member.
risk: More engineering and batching complexity than another tree model; gains depend on having enough grouped context signal per lap.
